text:
Release Notes - Version 3.2.0
Released: June 13, 2026
Overview
This release brings significant performance improvements, enhanced authentication features, and 
fixes for critical security vulnerabilities. We recommend all users upgrade as soon as possible.
Breaking Changes
• Removed deprecated api/v1/legacy endpoints. Please migrate to api/v2 (migration 
guide available at docs/migration-v1-to-v2.md).
• AuthToken expiration now defaults to 24 hours instead of 7 days. Use tokenExpiry

• AuthToken expiration now defaults to 24 hours instead of 7 days. Use tokenExpiry 
environment variable to configure.
• The --legacy-config CLI flag has been removed. Convert your config to the new 
YAML format using migrate-config tool.
New Features
Authentication & Security
• Multi-factor Authentication (MFA): Users can now enable time-based one-time password 
(TOTP) for enhanced account security.
• OAuth2 Integration: Added support for login via Google, GitHub, and Microsoft accounts.

• OAuth2 Integration: Added support for login via Google, GitHub, and Microsoft accounts. 
Enterprise customers can configure custom OAuth2 providers.
• IP Whitelist Management: New dashboard controls to manage allowed IP ranges for API 
access. Per-user and per-application granularity supported.
• Security Headers: Automatic HSTS, CSP, and X-Frame-Options headers added to all 
HTTP responses.
Performance Enhancements

HTTP responses.
Performance Enhancements
• Database Query Optimization: Reduced average query time by 40% through index 
improvements and query rewrites.
• Caching Layer: Redis-backed distributed cache for frequently accessed resources. TTL 
configurable per resource type.
• Connection Pooling: Increased default connection pool size from 10 to 50. Adaptive pool 
sizing under heavy load.
• Batch API Operations: New /batch endpoint for submitting up to 100 operations in a

• Batch API Operations: New /batch endpoint for submitting up to 100 operations in a 
single request. Useful for bulk data migrations.

API Improvements
• GraphQL Endpoint: New /graphql endpoint alongside REST API. Full schema 
documentation available in the API explorer.
• Webhook Events: Added webhook support for user creation, deletion, and role changes. 
Retry logic with exponential backoff.
• Rate Limiting: Improved rate limiter now distinguishes between authenticated and 
unauthenticated requests. See docs for new limits.
• Response Pagination: New cursor-based pagination (in addition to offset) for large result 
sets.

• Response Pagination: New cursor-based pagination (in addition to offset) for large result 
sets.
User Interface
• Dark Mode: Complete dark mode theme with automatic OS preference detection.
• Improved Dashboard: Redesigned dashboard with real-time metrics, customizable 
widgets, and export functionality.
• Mobile Responsive: All pages now 100% mobile responsive. Tested across iOS 14+ and 
Android 10+.
• Accessibility: Added ARIA labels, improved keyboard navigation, and screen reader

Android 10+.
• Accessibility: Added ARIA labels, improved keyboard navigation, and screen reader 
support (WCAG 2.1 AA compliant).
Developer Experience
• SDKs: Released official SDKs for Python, JavaScript, Go, and Java with full type hints and 
autocomplete support.
• CLI Tool: New command-line tool for local development. Replaces separate shell scripts. 
Supports Windows, macOS, Linux.
• Improved Logging: Structured JSON logging with configurable verbosity levels. Integrates

• Improved Logging: Structured JSON logging with configurable verbosity levels. Integrates 
with ELK/Splunk/DataDog.
• OpenTelemetry Support: Full instrumentation for tracing, metrics, and logs. Tested with 
Jaeger and Zipkin.
Bug Fixes
• Fixed: User session not persisting across page reloads (issue #4521)
• Fixed: Race condition in concurrent update scenario causing data corruption (issue #4892)
• Fixed: CSV export truncating fields over 255 characters (issue #4133)

• Fixed: CSV export truncating fields over 255 characters (issue #4133)
• Fixed: Timezone handling in scheduled tasks not respecting DST (issue #4456)
• Fixed: Memory leak in background worker process after 7 days uptime (issue #4701)
• Fixed: Email notifications not sent to users with plus-addressed emails (issue #4322)

Deprecated Features
• api/v2/users/search endpoint (use api/v3/users/search instead). Will be 
removed in v4.0.
• Database driver for PostgreSQL 9.6 compatibility (upgrade to 12+ recommended).
• LDAP authentication (OAuth2 recommended as replacement). Still functional but 
unsupported.
Known Limitations
• GraphQL endpoint does not yet support subscriptions (will be added in v3.3).
• Webhook retry mechanism has a maximum of 10 attempts over 24 hours.

• Webhook retry mechanism has a maximum of 10 attempts over 24 hours.
• Dark mode theme colors not yet customizable (planned for v4.0).
Performance Metrics
• Average response time: 120ms (down from 200ms)
• Database query time: 25ms (down from 45ms)
• Memory usage per instance: 256MB (down from 380MB)
• Throughput: ~5,000 requests/second (up from ~2,500)
Deployment & Upgrades
• Docker Image: Published to Docker Hub at mycompany/myapp:3.2.0. Size: 145MB.

• Docker Image: Published to Docker Hub at mycompany/myapp:3.2.0. Size: 145MB.
• Kubernetes Helm Chart: Updated chart available at charts/myapp:3.2.0.
• Database Migration: Run migrate.sh to update schema. Estimated time: 10 minutes for 
typical databases.
• Backward Compatibility: v3.1.x clients will continue to work with v3.2.0 servers, but new 
features require client upgrade.
Dependencies Updated
• Framework: Updated from 2.4 to 3.1 (minor breaking changes; see migration guide).

• Framework: Updated from 2.4 to 3.1 (minor breaking changes; see migration guide).
• Database driver: Updated from 1.9 to 2.3 (now supports async queries).
• Authentication library: Updated to support TOTP; TOTP QR codes now generated client-
side.
• Encryption library: Updated from Crypto 3.x to 4.x (security patch for AES mode).
Contributors
Thanks to everyone who contributed to this release, including:
• Sarah Chen (Performance improvements)

• Mike Rodriguez (OAuth2 integration)
• Emily Watson (Mobile responsive design)
• Team Infrastructure (Deployment automation)
Support & Documentation
• Docs: https://docs.mycompany.com/3.2
• Migration Guide: https://docs.mycompany.com/3.2/migration-from-3.1
• API Reference: https://api.mycompany.com/docs (interactive explorer)
• Support: Email support@mycompany.com or visit https://support.mycompany.com
Download
• Windows x64  
• macOS  
• Linux x64  
• Docker

• macOS  
• Linux x64  
• Docker  
Security Notice: Please review the security advisories section on our website. Version 3.2.0 patches 
3 critical vulnerabilities from v3.1.x.
